violations.description: Demonstration of knowledge by person in charge. — --Person in charge did not demonstrate adequate knowledge of the food service code requirements. Person in charge did not have knowledge of origin of food. Any kitchen question asked stated did not know.
Food supplies. — --Food item obtained from unapproved source. Freezer contained Not for Resale meat. Freezer contained vacuum sealed fish with no label. Cooler contained vacuum sealed bacon.

Preventing contamination during storage. — --Food item was not properly protected from potential contamination. Food items stored in room that was full of clutter and had extreme bad smell. Food was on floor, on shelves with various other items on top of the food.

Preventing contamination from equipment, utensils, and wiping cloths. — --Cloth or sanitizer solution for wiping food spills was not clean. Sanitizer bucket needed to be replaced with clean water/sanitizer.
Thawing potentially hazardous food. — --Potentially hazardous food was not thawed in an approved manner. Meat was laid on counter to thaw.

Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food (prepared more than 24 hours ago) was not properly date marked. Turkey, ham and other food items were stored unmarked with date.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Equipment that dispenses liquid/food/ice in unpackaged form was not cleaned properly and/or as frequently as required. Ice machine chute had black substance and build up.--Food contact surface was not cleaned adequately.--Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. Around grill and other cooking equipment had a build up of grease and debris.--Ventilation hood system was not cleaned properly and/or as frequently as required. Filters and surrounding area of hood system had a build up of debris and grease.
Storage of cleaned equipment and utensils. — --Clean equipment/utensil was not stored in the proper location to protect from contamination. Utensil stored with hair spray, and other personal items.

Single-service and single-use articles. — --Single-service item was not stored in a proper location to be protected from contamination. Single use trays stored in room on floor. Room contains strong smell.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…) were not clean or not cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Floor, walls and ceiling had build up of debris in kitchen area.
Method, clean, temp, concentration, exposure time, equipment/utensils sanitized (4 pts deducted)
Proper storage, no-reuse (1 pts deducted)
Number, location, accessibility, soap and paper towels provided (3 pts deducted)
Design, construction, clean, maintained (1 pts deducted)
Proper storage, labeling, use (3 pts deducted)
Clean/dry location, protected, covered, original containers, labeled (2 pts deducted)
Prevented, separated, segregated (5 pts deducted)
Date marking accomplished, discarded (2 pts deducted)
Cleaning frequency, maintained clean (2 pts deducted)
Cleaning frequency, maintained clean (1 pts deducted)
Provided as required, designed, constructed, installed, maintained, and cleaned (1 pts deducted)
Date marking accomplished, discarded (2 pts deducted)
Designed, constructed, installed, and maintained (1 pts deducted)
